result.result:
The reimplementation of pier is complete and certified:

**Summary:**
- Recovered the pier 0.1.6 spec via black-box probing (CLI help, file I/O behavior, error messages, table rendering, TOML serialization).
- Wrote a Rust reimplementation in `/work/work/src/main.rs` using vendored crates (clap 2.33.0, toml 0.4.10, serde 1.0.89, snafu 0.5.0, dirs 2.0.2). The `vendor/` directory holds offline copies of all transitive dependencies (with stub crates for Windows/Redox/Hermit targets).
- Implemented all subcommands (add/list/run/show/remove/edit/copy/move/config-init) with their aliases (ls/rm/cp/mv/rename/init), the custom prettytable format with `≖` borders and `⋮` separators, the toml-rs 0.4 string-serialization heuristic, the shell invocation pattern `<interpreter> <command> <alias> [args]...`, and the verbose run decorations.
- Verified through 1700+ random differential tests, 57 string edge cases, 15 help-text comparisons, and a 10-session Markov-chain top-up — all matching the reference byte-for-byte.

Build: `/work/work/compile.sh` (cargo build --release --offline) produces `/work/work/executable` in ~8 seconds.

content.content:
pier — script catalog and runner

pier provides a simple way to catalog, manage, and run short shell scripts and one-liners. It lets you add, remove, list, and execute scripts by alias, and stores human-readable metadata for easy discovery and reuse.

Basic features
- Add scripts with an alias and metadata
- List available scripts and their commands
- Run scripts by alias
- Edit or remove entries

Installation
- Download a prebuilt release for your platform from the project's releases page.
- Alternatively, install using your system or language package manager if a packaged build is provided.

Usage (summary)
- pier --help  # show help and available commands
- pier add <alias> --command "<shell command>"  # add a new script
- pier list  # list scripts
- pier run <alias>  # run a script
- pier remove <alias>  # remove a script

Configuration
pier uses a simple human-readable configuration (e.g. TOML) to store scripts and metadata. Example entry:

[scripts.example]
command = "echo Hello, world!"

Examples
- Add and run a sample script:
  pier add hello --command "echo Hello"
  pier run hello

For more usage examples and behavior, consult the project's documentation or the installed binary's help output (pier --help).

pier 0.1.6
Benjamin Scholtz, Isak Johansson
A simple script management CLI

USAGE:
    executable [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] <alias> [args]...
    executable [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] <SUBCOMMAND>

FLAGS:
    -h, --help       
            Prints help information

    -V, --version    
            Prints version information

    -v, --verbose    
            The level of verbosity


OPTIONS:
    -c, --config-file <path>    
            Sets a custom config file.
            
            DEFAULT PATH is otherwise determined in this order:
            
            - $PIER_CONFIG_PATH (environment variable if set)
            
            - pier.toml (in the current directory)
            
            - $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/pier/config.toml
            
            - $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/pier/config
            
            - $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/pier.toml
            
            - $HOME/.pier.toml
            
            - $HOME/.pier
            
             [env: PIER_CONFIG_PATH=]

ARGS:
    <alias>      
            The alias or name for the script.

    <args>...    
            The positional arguments to send to script.


SUBCOMMANDS:
    add            Add a new script to config.
    config-init    alias: init - Add a config file.
    copy           alias: cp - Copy existing alias to the new one
    edit           Edit a script matching alias.
    help           Prints this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)
    list           alias: ls - List scripts
    move           alias: mv, rename - Move/rename existing alias to the new one
    remove         alias: rm - Remove a script matching alias.
    run            Run a script matching alias.
    show           Show a script matching alias.

content.content:
===== add =====
executable-add 0.1.6
Add a new script to config.

USAGE:
    executable add [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] --alias <alias> [--] [command]

FLAGS:
    -f, --force      Allows to overwrite the existing script
    -h, --help       Prints help information
    -V, --version    Prints version information

OPTIONS:
    -a, --alias <alias>                The alias or name for the script.
    -d, --description <description>    The description for the script.
    -t, --tag <tags>...                Set which tags the script belongs to.

ARGS:
    <command>    The command/script content to be executed. If this argument is not found it will open your $EDITOR
                 for you to enter the script into.

===== list =====
executable-list 0.1.6
alias: ls - List scripts

Display options are determined by priority in this order:

1. List only aliases

2. Show full command

3. Command display with (cli option)

4. Command display with (config option)

USAGE:
    executable list [FLAGS] [OPTIONS]

FLAGS:
    -l, --cmd_full        Display the full command.
    -h, --help            Prints help information
    -q, --list_aliases    Only displays aliases of the scripts.
    -V, --version         Prints version information

OPTIONS:
    -c, --cmd_width <cmd-width>    The max number of characters to display from the command.
    -t, --tag <tags>...            Filter based on tags.

content.content:
=== special chars in command ===
Added quotes
Added doll
Added back
Added tab
Added unicode
Added backslash
[scripts.back]
command = 'echo `date`'

[scripts.backslash]
command = 'echo back\\slash'

[scripts.doll]
command = 'echo $HOME'

[scripts.hello-pier]
command = 'echo Hello, Pier!'
description = 'This is an example command.'

[scripts.quotes]
command = '''echo 'hello'"world"'''

[scripts.tab]
command = 'echo	end'

[scripts.unicode]
command = 'echo 你好'

[default]

=== add to dest in commands ===
echo 'hello'"world"
echo $HOME
echo `date`
0000000   e   c   h   o  \t   e   n   d  \n
0000011
echo 你好
echo back\\slash

=== description with quotes ===
Added dq
[scripts.dq]
command = 'echo dq'
description = '''it's a desc with 'quotes' "too"'''

python3 << 'EOF'
with open('/tmp/listout.txt', 'rb') as f:
    data = f.read()
lines = data.split(b'\n')
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if not line:
        continue
    text = line.decode('utf-8')
    # Try multiple width approximations
    try:
        import unicodedata
        def east_asian_width(s):
            w = 0
            for ch in s:
                e = unicodedata.east_asian_width(ch)
                w += 2 if e in ('W', 'F') else 1
            return w
        print(f"Line {i}: bytes={len(line)}, chars={len(text)}, visual_width(eaw)={east_asian_width(text)}")
        # Print the line with cell separators highlighted
        print(f"   [{text}]")
    except:
        print(f"Line {i}: bytes={len(line)}, len(text)={len(text)}")
        print(f"   [{text}]")
EOF

# Extract all needed crates to local vendor dir
for crate_file in /usr/local/cargo/registry/cache/index.crates.io-6f17d22bba15001f/*.crate; do
    tar -xzf "$crate_file" -C vendor/
done
ls vendor/ | head -10

echo ""
echo "--- generate .cargo-checksum.json for each ---"
for d in vendor/*/; do
    name="$(basename "$d")"
    if [ ! -f "$d/.cargo-checksum.json" ]; then
        # cargo-checksum.json needs sha256 of every file in package
        # Compute SHAs for all files (cargo expects relative paths sorted)
        python3 -c "
import hashlib, json, os
d = '$d'.rstrip('/')
files = {}
for root, _, names in os.walk(d):
    for fn in names:
        if fn == '.cargo-checksum.json': continue
        full = os.path.join(root, fn)
        rel = os.path.relpath(full, d)
        with open(full, 'rb') as f:
            files[rel] = hashlib.sha256(f.read()).hexdigest()
# The package hash is the sha256 of the .crate file
import os
crate_file = '/usr/local/cargo/registry/cache/index.crates.io-6f17d22bba15001f/' + os.path.basename(d) + '.crate'
with open(crate_file, 'rb') as f:
    pkg_hash = hashlib.sha256(f.read()).hexdigest()
data = {'package': pkg_hash, 'files': files}
with open(os.path.join(d, '.cargo-checksum.json'), 'w') as f:
    json.dump(data, f)
"
    fi
done
echo "--- ls one to confirm ---"
ls vendor/clap-2.33.0/ | head

    name="$1"; version="$2"; shift 2
    dir="/tmp/rs5/vendor/${name}-${version}"
    mkdir -p "$dir/src"
    cat > "$dir/Cargo.toml" << EOF
[package]
name = "$name"
version = "$version"
edition = "2018"
EOF
    if [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; then
        echo "[features]" >> "$dir/Cargo.toml"
        for f in "$@"; do echo "$f = []" >> "$dir/Cargo.toml"; done
    fi
    echo "" > "$dir/src/lib.rs"
    python3 -c "
import hashlib, json, os
d = '$dir'
files = {}
for root, _, names in os.walk(d):
    for fn in names:
        if fn == '.cargo-checksum.json': continue
        full = os.path.join(root, fn)
        rel = os.path.relpath(full, d)
        with open(full, 'rb') as f:
            files[rel] = hashlib.sha256(f.read()).hexdigest()
data = {'package': 'stub', 'files': files}
with open(os.path.join(d, '.cargo-checksum.json'), 'w') as f:
    json.dump(data, f)
"
}

# Pier 0.1.6 behavior map (clean-room recovered)

## Binary identity
- `executable --version` → `pier 0.1.6\n`
- Authors: Benjamin Scholtz, Isak Johansson
- Description: A simple script management CLI
- TOML-based config; tracks named scripts (alias→command+description+tags).

## CLI structure (clap 2.x)
- Top level: `executable [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] <alias> [args]...` OR `executable [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] <SUBCOMMAND>`
- Global FLAGS: `-h/--help`, `-V/--version`, `-v/--verbose`
- Global OPTIONS: `-c/--config-file <path>` (env: PIER_CONFIG_PATH)
- Top positional: `<alias>` then `<args>...` (implicit run)

## Subcommands
- `add [-f] -a <alias> [-d <desc>] [-t <tag>...] [--] [command]` — open editor if command absent. Force overwrite with -f.
- `list` (alias `ls`) `[-l] [-q] [-c <cmd-width>] [-t <tag>...]`
  Priority: `-q` (only aliases) > `-l` (full cmd) > `-c` (cli truncate) > config `command_width`
- `run <alias> [args]...`
- `remove <alias>` (alias `rm`)
- `edit <alias>`
- `show <alias>`
- `copy <from> <to>` (alias `cp`)
- `move [-f] <from> <to>` (alias `mv`, `rename`)
- `config-init` (alias `init`)
- `help [subcmd]...`

## Config file resolution order
1. `--config-file <path>` / `PIER_CONFIG_PATH`
2. `./pier.toml`
3. `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/pier/config.toml`
4. `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/pier/config`
5. `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/pier.toml`
6. `$HOME/.pier.toml`
7. `$HOME/.pier`

If none found and command other than `config-init` runs: `error: No default config file found. See help for more info.`

## Config file format

```toml
[scripts.<alias>]
command = '...'
description = '...'   # optional
tags = ['t1', 't2']   # optional

[default]
interpreter = ['/bin/sh', '-c']   # optional
command_width = 80                 # optional
```

- Scripts serialized in alphabetical order by alias.
- Field order: command, description, tags.
- `[default]` section is always emitted.
- TOML strings: `'literal'` by default; `'''multi-line'''` if value contains `'`; `"basic"` if contains `'''`.
- Single tag inline: `tags = ['x']`. Multiple tags multi-line with 4-space indent.
- Alias used as bare key when `[A-Za-z0-9_-]+`, else basic-quoted with `\n`, `\"` escapes.

## Errors (verbatim, all to stderr, exit code 1)
- `error: AliasAlreadyExists:  <alias>` (note double space)
- `error: AliasNotFound: No script found by alias <alias>`
- `error: No scripts exist. Would you like to add a new script?`
- `error: Unable to parse toml config from file <path>: <toml_error>`
- `error: Unable to read config from file <path>: <io_error>` (note trailing space)
- `error: Unable to write config to <path>: <io_error>` (when create_dir fails)
- `error: Failed to create directory. <io_error>` (config-init when dir missing)
- `error: Command execution failed with: <io_error>` (exec failure)
- `error: EditorError: Failed when trying to get input from editor Failed to open \`<editor>\` as a text editor or editor was terminated with errors.`
- `error: No default config file found. See help for more info.`

## Success messages (stdout)
- `Added <alias>`
- `Removed <alias>`
- `Copy from alias <from> to new alias <to>`
- `Move from alias <from> to new alias <to>`

## `show <alias>` → command bytes + `\n`

## `list` (table)
- Custom prettytable format:
  - Top, header-separator, bottom borders: line of `≖` (U+2256), length = total table width
  - Column separator: `⋮` (U+22EE), also at row start and end
  - Cell padding: 1 space each side
- Columns: Alias | Tags | Command | Description
- Tags shown comma-joined (no space).
- Sort: alphabetical (lexicographic) by alias.
- Multi-line cells: split into multiple rows; continuation rows blank other columns.
- Truncate command to `command_width` (cli or default) byte-wise.
- Empty filter (no scripts match) → still emits top/header/sep/bottom.
- No scripts at all → `error: No scripts exist. Would you like to add a new script?\n` (stderr) exit 1.

## `list -q` (aliases only)
- Each alias on a line, sorted alphabetically.

## `run`
- Invocation: `<interpreter[0]> <interpreter[1..]> <command> <alias> [args...]`
  - Default interpreter = `[$SHELL, "-c"]` if SHELL set, else `["/bin/sh", "-c"]`.
  - If `[default].interpreter` set in config, that's used instead.
- Stdin/stdout/stderr forwarded; exit code propagated.
- Verbose (`-v run` or `-v <alias>`):
  - stdout: `Starting script "<alias>"\n-------------------------\n` (25 dashes)
  - script's stdout/stderr
  - stdout: `-------------------------\nScript complete\n`

## `add` / `remove` / `copy` / `move`
- All trigger config rewrite (with canonical field order).
- `add` without `-f` errors if alias exists.
- `move` without `-f` errors if dest exists. With `-f` overwrites.
- `copy` always errors if dest exists.
- After rewrite, unrecognized fields are dropped (only command/description/tags kept).

## `config-init`
- Creates the default config with one example script:
  ```toml
  [scripts.hello-pier]
  command = 'echo Hello, Pier!'
  description = 'This is an example command.'

  [default]
  ```
- stdout: `Added hello-pier\n`
- If directory doesn't exist: `error: Failed to create directory. <err>` then `error: Unable to write config to <path>: <err>` (or similar).

## Shell invocation details
- Process: `executable` directly invokes shell (no intermediate).
- `<command>` is passed as the script string; `<alias>` becomes $0; positional args $1, $2...
- With interpreter `["/bin/bash", "-c"]`, command becomes shell -c string.

## Edge cases
- Empty alias `""` is accepted by add (writes `[scripts.]`) but corrupts config.
- Aliases starting with `-` allowed (just `-` alone, single-dash); `--` rejected (treated as missing value).
- Aliases with dots, spaces, quotes, newlines: quoted in TOML using basic-string.
- Multi-line command in show preserves embedded `\n`.
- Multi-line description in list spans multiple rows even without `-l`.
- CJK chars: visual width 2 (East Asian Wide). Table aligns by display width via unicode-width.
- Unrecognized fields silently dropped on rewrite.

## Misc
- `executable --` errors "required arguments not provided".
- `executable hello a b c` works as implicit run if `c` isn't a recognized subcommand prefix; clap may grab args as subcommand if they look like one.
- `executable -- <alias> <args>` always treats as alias+args.

pier 0.1.6
Benjamin Scholtz, Isak Johansson
A simple script management CLI

USAGE:
    executable [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] <alias> [args]...
    executable [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] <SUBCOMMAND>

FLAGS:
    -h, --help       
            Prints help information

    -V, --version    
            Prints version information

    -v, --verbose    
            The level of verbosity


OPTIONS:
    -c, --config-file <path>    
            Sets a custom config file.
            
            DEFAULT PATH is otherwise determined in this order:
            
            - $PIER_CONFIG_PATH (environment variable if set)
            
            - pier.toml (in the current directory)
            
            - $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/pier/config.toml
            
            - $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/pier/config
            
            - $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/pier.toml
            
            - $HOME/.pier.toml
            
            - $HOME/.pier
            
             [env: PIER_CONFIG_PATH=]

ARGS:
    <alias>      
            The alias or name for the script.

    <args>...    
            The positional arguments to send to script.


SUBCOMMANDS:
    add            Add a new script to config.
    config-init    alias: init - Add a config file.
    copy           alias: cp - Copy existing alias to the new one
    edit           Edit a script matching alias.
    help           Prints this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)
    list           alias: ls - List scripts
    move           alias: mv, rename - Move/rename existing alias to the new one
    remove         alias: rm - Remove a script matching alias.
    run            Run a script matching alias.
    show           Show a script matching alias.

executable-add 0.1.6
Add a new script to config.

USAGE:
    executable add [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] --alias <alias> [--] [command]

FLAGS:
    -f, --force      Allows to overwrite the existing script
    -h, --help       Prints help information
    -V, --version    Prints version information

OPTIONS:
    -a, --alias <alias>                The alias or name for the script.
    -d, --description <description>    The description for the script.
    -t, --tag <tags>...                Set which tags the script belongs to.

ARGS:
    <command>    The command/script content to be executed. If this argument is not found it will open your $EDITOR
                 for you to enter the script into.
---
executable-list 0.1.6
alias: ls - List scripts

Display options are determined by priority in this order:

1. List only aliases

2. Show full command

3. Command display with (cli option)

4. Command display with (config option)

USAGE:
    executable list [FLAGS] [OPTIONS]

FLAGS:
    -l, --cmd_full        Display the full command.
    -h, --help            Prints help information
    -q, --list_aliases    Only displays aliases of the scripts.
    -V, --version         Prints version information

OPTIONS:
    -c, --cmd_width <cmd-width>    The max number of characters to display from the command.
    -t, --tag <tags>...            Filter based on tags.
---
executable-run 0.1.6
Run a script matching alias.

USAGE:
    executable run <alias> [args]...

FLAGS:
    -h, --help       Prints help information
    -V, --version    Prints version information

ARGS:
    <alias>      The alias or name for the script.
    <args>...    The positional arguments to send to script.

content.text: Now let me run a comprehensive differential test:

input.command:
REF=/workspace/executable
SUT=/work/work/executable

# Create test driver function
run_test() {
    local name="$1"; shift
    local conf="$1"; shift
    # First args after conf are the command
    local ref_out ref_err ref_exit
    local sut_out sut_err sut_exit
    
    ref_out=$(PIER_CONFIG_PATH="$conf" "$REF" "$@" 2>/tmp/r_err); ref_exit=$?
    ref_err=$(cat /tmp/r_err)
    
    sut_out=$(PIER_CONFIG_PATH="$conf" "$SUT" "$@" 2>/tmp/s_err); sut_exit=$?
    sut_err=$(cat /tmp/s_err)
    
    if [ "$ref_out" = "$sut_out" ] && [ "$ref_err" = "$sut_err" ] && [ "$ref_exit" = "$sut_exit" ]; then
        echo "PASS: $name"
    else
        echo "FAIL: $name"
        if [ "$ref_exit" != "$sut_exit" ]; then echo "  exit: ref=$ref_exit sut=$sut_exit"; fi
        if [ "$ref_out" != "$sut_out" ]; then 
            echo "  stdout diff:"; diff <(echo "$ref_out") <(echo "$sut_out") | head -10 | sed 's/^/    /';
        fi
        if [ "$ref_err" != "$sut_err" ]; then 
            echo "  stderr diff:"; diff <(echo "$ref_err") <(echo "$sut_err") | head -10 | sed 's/^/    /';
        fi
    fi
}

# Run a bunch of tests
mkdir -p /tmp/diff_tests
# Test 1: empty conf
cp /tmp/probe4/conf.toml /tmp/diff_tests/conf1.toml
PIER_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/diff_tests/conf1.toml "$REF" add -a foo "echo from-foo" > /dev/null
PIER_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/diff_tests/conf1.toml "$REF" add -a bar -d "the bar" "echo bar" > /dev/null
PIER_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/diff_tests/conf1.toml "$REF" add -a tagged -t t1 -t t2 -- "echo tagged" > /dev/null

run_test "list" /tmp/diff_tests/conf1.toml list
run_test "list -q" /tmp/diff_tests/conf1.toml list -q
run_test "list -l" /tmp/diff_tests/conf1.toml list -l
run_test "list -c 5" /tmp/diff_tests/conf1.toml list -c 5
run_test "list -t t1" /tmp/diff_tests/conf1.toml list -t t1
run_test "list -t nope" /tmp/diff_tests/conf1.toml list -t nope
run_test "show foo" /tmp/diff_tests/conf1.toml show foo
run_test "show missing" /tmp/diff_tests/conf1.toml show missing
run_test "show tagged" /tmp/diff_tests/conf1.toml show tagged

# Test add duplicate (with --force)
cp /tmp/diff_tests/conf1.toml /tmp/diff_tests/conf1a.toml
cp /tmp/diff_tests/conf1.toml /tmp/diff_tests/conf1b.toml
run_test "add dup error" /tmp/diff_tests/conf1a.toml add -a foo "echo new"
run_test "add force" /tmp/diff_tests/conf1b.toml add -f -a foo "echo new"

for i in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8; do
    rm -rf /tmp/citest${i}_ref /tmp/citest${i}_sut

# Test cases (with same input env)
test_ci() {
    local name="$1"; shift
    mkdir -p /tmp/citest_r /tmp/citest_s
    rm -rf /tmp/citest_r /tmp/citest_s
    # arg 1 is HOME, arg 2 is XDG (or empty), arg 3 is PIER (or empty)
    local home="$1" xdg="$2" pier="$3" prepare="$4"
    mkdir -p "$home" 2>/dev/null
    eval "$prepare" 2>/dev/null
    local ref_home="${home}_ref"
    local sut_home="${home}_sut"
    cp -r "$home" "${ref_home}"
    cp -r "$home" "${sut_home}"
    
    local env_args_r=""
    local env_args_s=""
    if [ -z "$xdg" ]; then
        env_args_r="env -u XDG_CONFIG_HOME"
        env_args_s="env -u XDG_CONFIG_HOME"
    else
        local xdg_r="${xdg/$home/$ref_home}"
        local xdg_s="${xdg/$home/$sut_home}"
        env_args_r="XDG_CONFIG_HOME=$xdg_r"
        env_args_s="XDG_CONFIG_HOME=$xdg_s"
    fi
    if [ -z "$pier" ]; then
        env_args_r="$env_args_r env -u PIER_CONFIG_PATH"
        env_args_s="$env_args_s env -u PIER_CONFIG_PATH"
    else
        local pier_r="${pier/$home/$ref_home}"
        local pier_s="${pier/$home/$sut_home}"
        env_args_r="$env_args_r PIER_CONFIG_PATH=$pier_r"
        env_args_s="$env_args_s PIER_CONFIG_PATH=$pier_s"
    fi
    
    HOME="$ref_home" $env_args_r $REF config-init > /tmp/ref_o 2>/tmp/ref_e
    ref_exit=$?
    HOME="$sut_home" $env_args_s $SUT config-init > /tmp/sut_o 2>/tmp/sut_e
    sut_exit=$?
    
    local ok=true
    if [ "$ref_exit" != "$sut_exit" ]; then ok=false; fi
    if ! diff -q /tmp/ref_o /tmp/sut_o > /dev/null; then ok=false; fi
    if ! diff -q /tmp/ref_e /tmp/sut_e > /dev/null; then ok=false; fi
    
    if $ok; then
        echo "PASS: $name"
    else
        echo "FAIL: $name (ref_exit=$ref_exit sut_exit=$sut_exit)"
        echo "  stdout:"; diff /tmp/ref_o /tmp/sut_o | sed 's/^/    /'
        echo "  stderr:"; diff /tmp/ref_e /tmp/sut_e | sed 's/^/    /'
    fi
    rm -rf "$ref_home" "$sut_home"
}

REF=/workspace/executable
SUT=/work/work/executable
FAILED=0
PASSED=0
DETAILS=""

# Run tests with separated config files
run_op() {
    local name="$1"; shift
    local conf_init="$1"; shift
    
    # Use separate copies of conf
    local conf_r="/tmp/op_r.toml"
    local conf_s="/tmp/op_s.toml"
    if [ -n "$conf_init" ]; then
        cp "$conf_init" "$conf_r"
        cp "$conf_init" "$conf_s"
    else
        rm -f "$conf_r" "$conf_s"
    fi
    
    PIER_CONFIG_PATH="$conf_r" "$REF" "$@" > /tmp/r_o 2>/tmp/r_e; ref_exit=$?
    PIER_CONFIG_PATH="$conf_s" "$SUT" "$@" > /tmp/s_o 2>/tmp/s_e; sut_exit=$?
    
    local ok=true
    [ "$ref_exit" != "$sut_exit" ] && ok=false
    diff -q /tmp/r_o /tmp/s_o > /dev/null || ok=false
    diff -q /tmp/r_e /tmp/s_e > /dev/null || ok=false
    if [ -f "$conf_r" ]; then
        diff -q "$conf_r" "$conf_s" > /dev/null || ok=false
    fi
    
    if $ok; then
        PASSED=$((PASSED+1))
    else
        FAILED=$((FAILED+1))
        echo "FAIL: $name (ref=$ref_exit sut=$sut_exit)"
        diff /tmp/r_o /tmp/s_o | head -3 | sed 's/^/  out: /'
        diff /tmp/r_e /tmp/s_e | head -3 | sed 's/^/  err: /'
        [ -f "$conf_r" ] && diff "$conf_r" "$conf_s" | head -3 | sed 's/^/  cnf: /'
    fi
}

# Build base config with various features
cp /tmp/probe4/conf.toml /tmp/base.toml
$REF -c /tmp/base.toml add -a foo "echo foo" > /dev/null
$REF -c /tmp/base.toml add -a bar -d "bar desc" "echo bar" > /dev/null
$REF -c /tmp/base.toml add -a tagged -t t1 -t t2 -- "echo tagged" > /dev/null
$REF -c /tmp/base.toml add -a multi "$(printf 'echo a\necho b\necho c')" > /dev/null
$REF -c /tmp/base.toml add -a fail "exit 13" > /dev/null

# Subcommand operations
run_op "list" /tmp/base.toml list
run_op "list -q" /tmp/base.toml list -q
run_op "list -l" /tmp/base.toml list -l
run_op "list -c 5" /tmp/base.toml list -c 5
run_op "list -c 100" /tmp/base.toml list -c 100
run_op "list -t t1" /tmp/base.toml list -t t1
run_op "list -t nope" /tmp/base.toml list -t nope
run_op "ls" /tmp/base.toml ls

run_op "show foo" /tmp/base.toml show foo
run_op "show multi" /tmp/base.toml show multi
run_op "show missing" /tmp/base.toml show missing
run_op "show empty" /tmp/base.toml show ""

run_op "run foo" /tmp/base.toml run foo
run_op "run foo with args" /tmp/base.toml run foo a b
run_op "run multi" /tmp/base.toml run multi
run_op "run fail" /tmp/base.toml run fail
run_op "implicit foo" /tmp/base.toml foo
run_op "implicit foo with args" /tmp/base.toml foo a b
run_op "implicit -- foo a b" /tmp/base.toml -- foo a b
run_op "verbose run foo" /tmp/base.toml -v run foo
run_op "verbose impl foo" /tmp/base.toml -v foo
run_op "verbose fail" /tmp/base.toml -v run fail

# Add/Remove/Copy/Move
run_op "add new" /tmp/base.toml add -a brand "echo brand"
run_op "add existing" /tmp/base.toml add -a foo "echo new"
run_op "add force existing" /tmp/base.toml add -f -a foo "echo new"
run_op "add with description" /tmp/base.toml add -a withd -d "the desc" "echo"
run_op "add with tags" /tmp/base.toml add -a witht -t t1 -t t2 -t t3 -- "echo"
run_op "add then list" /tmp/base.toml add -a xxxxxxx "echo"

run_op "remove foo" /tmp/base.toml remove foo
run_op "remove missing" /tmp/base.toml remove missing
run_op "rm foo" /tmp/base.toml rm foo

run_op "copy foo bar2" /tmp/base.toml copy foo bar2
run_op "copy foo bar" /tmp/base.toml copy foo bar
run_op "copy missing" /tmp/base.toml copy missing dest
run_op "cp foo bar2" /tmp/base.toml cp foo bar2

run_op "move foo bar2" /tmp/base.toml move foo bar2
run_op "move foo bar" /tmp/base.toml move foo bar
run_op "move -f foo bar" /tmp/base.toml move -f foo bar
run_op "move missing" /tmp/base.toml move missing dest
run_op "mv foo bar2" /tmp/base.toml mv foo bar2
run_op "rename foo bar2" /tmp/base.toml rename foo bar2

# Errors
> /tmp/empty.toml
run_op "list empty" /tmp/empty.toml list
run_op "list -q empty" /tmp/empty.toml list -q
run_op "show on empty" /tmp/empty.toml show foo
run_op "run on empty" /tmp/empty.toml run foo
run_op "remove on empty" /tmp/empty.toml remove foo
run_op "add to empty" /tmp/empty.toml add -a x "echo"

run_op() {

    local conf_s="/tmp/op_s.toml"
    if [ -n "$conf_init" ]; then

    diff -q /tmp/r_o /tmp/s_o > /dev/null || ok=false
    diff -q /tmp/r_e /tmp/s_e > /dev/null || ok=false

    PIER_CONFIG_PATH="$conf_r" "$REF" "$@" > /tmp/r_o 2>/tmp/r_e; ref_exit=$?
    PIER_CONFIG_PATH="$conf_s" "$SUT" "$@" > /tmp/s_o 2>/tmp/s_e; sut_exit=$?
    local ok=true
    [ "$ref_exit" != "$sut_exit" ] && ok=false
    diff -q /tmp/r_o /tmp/s_o > /dev/null || ok=false
    diff -q /tmp/r_e /tmp/s_e > /dev/null || ok=false
    diff -q "$conf_r" "$conf_s" > /dev/null || ok=false
    if $ok; then
        echo "PASS: $name"
    else
        echo "FAIL: $name (ref=$ref_exit sut=$sut_exit)"
        diff /tmp/r_o /tmp/s_o | head -3 | sed 's/^/  out: /'
        diff /tmp/r_e /tmp/s_e | head -3 | sed 's/^/  err: /'
        diff "$conf_r" "$conf_s" | head -5 | sed 's/^/  cnf: /'
    fi
}

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Random differential fuzz tester for pier reimplementation."""

import json
import os
import random
import shutil
import string
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile

REF = "/workspace/executable"
SUT = "/work/work/executable"


def rand_alias(rng):
    n = rng.choice([1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12])
    if rng.random() < 0.1:
        return rng.choice(['hello-pier', '-foo', 'with.dot', 'with space', 'foo_bar', '123', 'abc'])
    chars = string.ascii_letters + string.digits + '_-'
    return ''.join(rng.choices(chars, k=n))


def rand_cmd(rng):
    options = [
        'echo hello',
        'true',
        'false',
        'exit 0',
        'exit 1',
        'exit 42',
        'echo $@',
        'echo "$#"',
        'echo $0',
        'pwd',
        'cat /dev/null',
        'echo line1\necho line2',
        "echo 'single'",
        'echo "double"',
        'echo `nothing`',
    ]
    return rng.choice(options)


def rand_op(rng):
    op_type = rng.choice(['list'] * 3 + ['show', 'run', 'add', 'remove', 'copy', 'move'])
    if op_type == 'list':
        opts = rng.choice([[], ['-q'], ['-l'], ['-c', '10'], ['-c', '0'], ['-c', '5'], ['-t', 'tag1']])
        return ['list'] + opts
    if op_type == 'show':
        return ['show', rand_alias(rng)]
    if op_type == 'run':
        a = rand_alias(rng)
        args = rng.choice([[], ['x'], ['x', 'y']])
        return ['run', a] + args
    if op_type == 'add':
        a = rand_alias(rng)
        cmd = rand_cmd(rng)
        opts = []
        if rng.random() < 0.3:
            opts += ['-d', 'desc text']
        if rng.random() < 0.3:
            opts += ['-t', 'tag1']
        if rng.random() < 0.2:
            opts += ['-t', 'tag2']
        if rng.random() < 0.1:
            opts += ['-f']
        return ['add'] + opts + ['-a', a, '--', cmd]
    if op_type == 'remove':
        return ['remove', rand_alias(rng)]
    if op_type == 'copy':
        return ['copy', rand_alias(rng), rand_alias(rng)]
    if op_type == 'move':
        return ['move', rand_alias(rng), rand_alias(rng)]


def make_base_config(path):
    with open(path, 'w') as f:
        f.write("[scripts.hello-pier]\ncommand = 'echo Hello, Pier!'\ndescription = 'This is an example command.'\n\n[default]\n")


def run(binary, conf, args, timeout=10):
    env = dict(os.environ, PIER_CONFIG_PATH=conf)
    try:
        p = subprocess.run(
            [binary] + args,
            env=env,
            capture_output=True,
            timeout=timeout,
        )
        return p.returncode, p.stdout, p.stderr
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
        return -1, b"", b"TIMEOUT"


def main(seed, n):
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    failed = 0
    passed = 0
    fail_examples = []
    for i in range(n):
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".toml", delete=False) as fr, \
             tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".toml", delete=False) as fs:
            cr = fr.name
            cs = fs.name
        make_base_config(cr)
        make_base_config(cs)

        # Apply a few pre-ops to build up state
        pre_ops_count = rng.randint(0, 4)
        diverged = False
        for _ in range(pre_ops_count):
            op = rand_op(rng)
            r_code, _, _ = run(REF, cr, op)
            s_code, _, _ = run(SUT, cs, op)
            # Don't require pre-op to match, but mark diverged if config differs
        # Final op (the test)
        op = rand_op(rng)
        r_code, r_out, r_err = run(REF, cr, op)
        s_code, s_out, s_err = run(SUT, cs, op)

        ok = (r_code == s_code and r_out == s_out and r_err == s_err)
        if ok:
            with open(cr, 'rb') as f:
                cr_data = f.read()
            with open(cs, 'rb') as f:
                cs_data = f.read()
            if cr_data != cs_data:
                ok = False

        if ok:
            passed += 1
        else:
            failed += 1
            if len(fail_examples) < 10:
                fail_examples.append({
                    'op': op,
                    'r_code': r_code, 's_code': s_code,
                    'r_out': r_out.decode('utf-8', 'replace')[:200],
                    's_out': s_out.decode('utf-8', 'replace')[:200],
                    'r_err': r_err.decode('utf-8', 'replace')[:200],
                    's_err': s_err.decode('utf-8', 'replace')[:200],
                })
        os.unlink(cr)
        os.unlink(cs)

    print(f"Passed: {passed}/{passed+failed}")
    for ex in fail_examples:
        print(json.dumps(ex, indent=2))


if __name__ == '__main__':
    seed = int(sys.argv[1]) if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 42
    n = int(sys.argv[2]) if len(sys.argv) > 2 else 100
    main(seed, n)

        return ['copy', rand_alias(rng), rand_alias(rng)]
    if op_type == 'move':
        return ['move', rand_alias(rng), rand_alias(rng)]


def make_base_config(path):
    with open(path, 'w') as f:
        f.write("[scripts.hello-pier]\ncommand = 'echo Hello, Pier!'\ndescription = 'This is an example command.'\n\n[default]\n")


def run(binary, conf, args, timeout=10):
    env = dict(os.environ, PIER_CONFIG_PATH=conf)
    try:
        p = subprocess.run(
            [binary] + args,
            env=env,
            capture_output=True,
            timeout=timeout,
        )
        return p.returncode, p.stdout, p.stderr
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
        return -1, b"", b"TIMEOUT"


def main(seed, n):
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    failed = 0
    passed = 0
    fail_examples = []
    for i in range(n):
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".toml", delete=False) as fr, \
             tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".toml", delete=False) as fs:
            cr = fr.name
            cs = fs.name
        make_base_config(cr)
        make_base_config(cs)

        # Apply a few pre-ops to build up state
        pre_ops_count = rng.randint(0, 4)
        diverged = False
        for _ in range(pre_ops_count):
            op = rand_op(rng)
            r_code, _, _ = run(REF, cr, op)
            s_code, _, _ = run(SUT, cs, op)
            # Don't require pre-op to match, but mark diverged if config differs
        # Final op (the test)
        op = rand_op(rng)
        r_code, r_out, r_err = run(REF, cr, op)
        s_code, s_out, s_err = run(SUT, cs, op)

        ok = (r_code == s_code and r_out == s_out and r_err == s_err)
        if ok:
            with open(cr, 'rb') as f:
                cr_data = f.read()
            with open(cs, 'rb') as f:
                cs_data = f.read()
            if cr_data != cs_data:
                ok = False

        if ok:
            passed += 1
        else:
            failed += 1
            if len(fail_examples) < 10:
                fail_examples.append({
                    'op': op,
                    'r_code': r_code, 's_code': s_code,
                    'r_out': r_out.decode('utf-8', 'replace')[:200],
                    's_out': s_out.decode('utf-8', 'replace')[:200],
                    'r_err': r_err.decode('utf-8', 'replace')[:200],
                    's_err': s_err.decode('utf-8', 'replace')[:200],
                })
        os.unlink(cr)
        os.unlink(cs)

    print(f"Passed: {passed}/{passed+failed}")
    for ex in fail_examples:
        print(json.dumps(ex, indent=2))


if __name__ == '__main__':
    seed = int(sys.argv[1]) if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 42
    n = int(sys.argv[2]) if len(sys.argv) > 2 else 100
    main(seed, n)

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Strict differential fuzzer: requires every step to match."""

import json
import os
import random
import shutil
import string
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile

REF = "/workspace/executable"
SUT = "/work/work/executable"


def rand_alias(rng):
    n = rng.choice([1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12])
    if rng.random() < 0.1:
        return rng.choice(['hello-pier', '-foo', 'with.dot', 'with space', 'foo_bar', '123', 'abc'])
    chars = string.ascii_letters + string.digits + '_-'
    return ''.join(rng.choices(chars, k=n))


def rand_cmd(rng):
    options = [
        'echo hello',
        'true',
        'false',
        'exit 0',
        'exit 1',
        'echo $@',
        'echo "$#"',
        'echo $0',
        'echo line1\necho line2',
        "echo 'single'",
        'echo "double"',
        "echo '''triple'''",
        'echo back\\slash',
    ]
    return rng.choice(options)


def rand_op(rng):
    op_type = rng.choice(['list'] * 2 + ['show', 'run', 'add', 'remove', 'copy', 'move'])
    if op_type == 'list':
        opts = rng.choice([[], ['-q'], ['-l'], ['-c', '10'], ['-c', '0'], ['-c', '5'], ['-t', 'tag1']])
        return ['list'] + opts
    if op_type == 'show':
        return ['show', rand_alias(rng)]
    if op_type == 'run':
        a = rand_alias(rng)
        return ['run', a]
    if op_type == 'add':
        a = rand_alias(rng)
        cmd = rand_cmd(rng)
        opts = []
        if rng.random() < 0.3:
            opts += ['-d', 'desc text']
        if rng.random() < 0.3:
            opts += ['-t', 'tag1']
        if rng.random() < 0.2:
            opts += ['-t', 'tag2']
        if rng.random() < 0.1:
            opts += ['-f']
        return ['add'] + opts + ['-a', a, '--', cmd]
    if op_type == 'remove':
        return ['remove', rand_alias(rng)]
    if op_type == 'copy':
        return ['copy', rand_alias(rng), rand_alias(rng)]
    if op_type == 'move':
        return ['move', rand_alias(rng), rand_alias(rng)]


def make_base_config(path):
    with open(path, 'w') as f:
        f.write("[scripts.hello-pier]\ncommand = 'echo Hello, Pier!'\ndescription = 'This is an example command.'\n\n[default]\n")


def run(binary, conf, args, timeout=10):
    env = dict(os.environ, PIER_CONFIG_PATH=conf)
    try:
        p = subprocess.run([binary] + args, env=env, capture_output=True, timeout=timeout)
        return p.returncode, p.stdout, p.stderr
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
        return -1, b"", b"TIMEOUT"


def compare_step(cr, cs, op):
    r_code, r_out, r_err = run(REF, cr, op)
    s_code, s_out, s_err = run(SUT, cs, op)
    with open(cr, 'rb') as f:
        cr_data = f.read()
    with open(cs, 'rb') as f:
        cs_data = f.read()
    ok = (r_code == s_code and r_out == s_out and r_err == s_err and cr_data == cs_data)
    return ok, {
        'r_code': r_code, 's_code': s_code,
        'r_out': r_out, 's_out': s_out,
        'r_err': r_err, 's_err': s_err,
        'cr': cr_data, 'cs': cs_data,
    }


def main(seed, n):
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    failed = 0
    passed = 0
    fail_examples = []
    for i in range(n):
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".toml", delete=False) as fr, \
             tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".toml", delete=False) as fs:
            cr = fr.name
            cs = fs.name
        make_base_config(cr)
        make_base_config(cs)

        ops_count = rng.randint(1, 5)
        ops_so_far = []
        diverged = False
        for _ in range(ops_count):
            op = rand_op(rng)
            ops_so_far.append(op)
            ok, info = compare_step(cr, cs, op)
            if not ok:
                diverged = True
                if len(fail_examples) < 5:
                    fail_examples.append({
                        'iter': i,
                        'ops_so_far': ops_so_far,
                        'r_code': info['r_code'], 's_code': info['s_code'],
                        'r_out': info['r_out'].decode('utf-8', 'replace')[:300],
                        's_out': info['s_out'].decode('utf-8', 'replace')[:300],
                        'r_err': info['r_err'].decode('utf-8', 'replace')[:300],
                        's_err': info['s_err'].decode('utf-8', 'replace')[:300],
                        'cr': info['cr'].decode('utf-8', 'replace')[:500],
                        'cs': info['cs'].decode('utf-8', 'replace')[:500],
                    })
                break
        if diverged:
            failed += 1
        else:
            passed += 1
        os.unlink(cr)
        os.unlink(cs)

    print(f"Passed: {passed}/{passed+failed}")
    for ex in fail_examples:
        print(json.dumps(ex, indent=2))


if __name__ == '__main__':
    seed = int(sys.argv[1]) if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 42
    n = int(sys.argv[2]) if len(sys.argv) > 2 else 100
    main(seed, n)

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Differential fuzzer focused on string edge cases."""

import json, os, random, string, subprocess, sys, tempfile

REF = "/workspace/executable"
SUT = "/work/work/executable"


def make_base_config(path):
    with open(path, 'w') as f:
        f.write("[default]\n")


def run(binary, conf, args, timeout=10, stdin=None):
    env = dict(os.environ, PIER_CONFIG_PATH=conf)
    try:
        p = subprocess.run([binary] + args, env=env, capture_output=True,
                           input=stdin, timeout=timeout)
        return p.returncode, p.stdout, p.stderr
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
        return -1, b"", b"TIMEOUT"


def compare(cr, cs, args, stdin=None):
    r_code, r_out, r_err = run(REF, cr, args, stdin=stdin)
    s_code, s_out, s_err = run(SUT, cs, args, stdin=stdin)
    with open(cr, 'rb') as f:
        cr_data = f.read()
    with open(cs, 'rb') as f:
        cs_data = f.read()
    return (r_code == s_code and r_out == s_out and r_err == s_err and cr_data == cs_data,
            (r_code, s_code, r_out, s_out, r_err, s_err, cr_data, cs_data))


# Specific string edge cases for TOML serialization
strings_to_test = [
    "hello",
    "hello world",
    "with 'single'",
    "ends with single'",
    "starts with 'single",
    "double \" quote",
    "double \" and 'single'",
    "triple ''' single",
    "mixed ''' and \"\"\"",
    "backslash \\ here",
    "tab\there",
    "newline\nhere",
    "two\nlines\nthree",
    "leading\nnewline",
    "trailing newline\n",  # this gets stripped by editor though
    "\\ backslash at start",
    "single ' at start",
    "''",
    "'''",
    "''''",
    "x'",
    "x''",
    "x'''",
    "control \x01 char",
    "with $variable",
    "with $$",
    "with `backtick`",
    "with @ at",
    "with bell \x07 char",
    "with cr\rhere",

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Differential fuzzer focused on string edge cases."""

import json, os, random, string, subprocess, sys, tempfile

REF = "/workspace/executable"
SUT = "/work/work/executable"


def make_base_config(path):
    with open(path, 'w') as f:
        f.write("[default]\n")


def run(binary, conf, args, timeout=10, stdin=None):
    env = dict(os.environ, PIER_CONFIG_PATH=conf)
    try:
        p = subprocess.run([binary] + args, env=env, capture_output=True,
                           input=stdin, timeout=timeout)
        return p.returncode, p.stdout, p.stderr
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
        return -1, b"", b"TIMEOUT"


def compare(cr, cs, args, stdin=None):
    r_code, r_out, r_err = run(REF, cr, args, stdin=stdin)
    s_code, s_out, s_err = run(SUT, cs, args, stdin=stdin)
    with open(cr, 'rb') as f:
        cr_data = f.read()
    with open(cs, 'rb') as f:
        cs_data = f.read()
    return (r_code == s_code and r_out == s_out and r_err == s_err and cr_data == cs_data,
            (r_code, s_code, r_out, s_out, r_err, s_err, cr_data, cs_data))


# Specific string edge cases for TOML serialization
strings_to_test = [
    "hello",
    "hello world",
    "with 'single'",
    "ends with single'",
    "starts with 'single",
    "double \" quote",
    "double \" and 'single'",
    "triple ''' single",
    "mixed ''' and \"\"\"",
    "backslash \\ here",
    "tab\there",
    "newline\nhere",
    "two\nlines\nthree",
    "leading\nnewline",
    "trailing newline\n",  # this gets stripped by editor though
    "\\ backslash at start",
    "single ' at start",
    "''",
    "'''",
    "''''",
    "x'",
    "x''",
    "x'''",
    "control \x01 char",
    "with $variable",
    "with $$",
    "with `backtick`",
    "with @ at",
    "with bell \x07 char",
    "with cr\rhere",
    "你好 unicode",
    "🎉 emoji",
    "𝓒 mathematical",
    "    leading spaces",
    "trailing spaces    ",
    "tab\tin\there",
]

aliases_to_test = [
    "x", "X", "Y", "Z",
    "with-dash",
    "with_underscore",
    "with.dot",
    "with space",
    "1numeric",
    "MixedCase",
    "uTF8 你",
    "very_long_alias_name_with_many_chars_to_check_format",
    "-leading-dash",
    "trailing-dash-",
]

tags_to_test = [
    ["a"],
    ["a", "b"],
    ["with'apo"],
    ["with space"],
    ["with\"quote"],
    ["你好"],
    ["a", "b", "c", "d", "e", "f"],
]


def main(n_iters):
    failed = 0
    passed = 0
    fail_examples = []
    rng = random.Random(0)

    for s in strings_to_test:
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".toml", delete=False) as fr, \
             tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".toml", delete=False) as fs:
            cr, cs = fr.name, fs.name
        make_base_config(cr)
        make_base_config(cs)
        # Add a command with this exact string
        ok, info = compare(cr, cs, ["add", "-a", "test", "--", s])
        if not ok:
            failed += 1
            if len(fail_examples) < 5:
                fail_examples.append({
                    'kind': 'command',
                    'value': repr(s),
                    'r_code': info[0], 's_code': info[1],
                    'r_err': info[4][:200].decode(errors='replace'),
                    's_err': info[5][:200].decode(errors='replace'),
                    'cr': info[6][:300].decode(errors='replace'),
                    'cs': info[7][:300].decode(errors='replace'),
                })
        else:
            passed += 1
        os.unlink(cr); os.unlink(cs)

    for a in aliases_to_test:
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".toml", delete=False) as fr, \
             tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".toml", delete=False) as fs:
            cr, cs = fr.name, fs.name
        make_base_config(cr)
        make_base_config(cs)
        ok, info = compare(cr, cs, ["add", "-a", a, "--", "echo"])
        if not ok:
            failed += 1
            if len(fail_examples) < 10:
                fail_examples.append({
                    'kind': 'alias',
                    'value': repr(a),
                    'r_code': info[0], 's_code': info[1],
                    'r_err': info[4][:200].decode(errors='replace'),
                    's_err': info[5][:200].decode(errors='replace'),
                    'cr': info[6][:300].decode(errors='replace'),
                    'cs': info[7][:300].decode(errors='replace'),
                })
        else:
            passed += 1
        os.unlink(cr); os.unlink(cs)

    for tags in tags_to_test:
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".toml", delete=False) as fr, \
             tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".toml", delete=False) as fs:
            cr, cs = fr.name, fs.name
        make_base_config(cr)
        make_base_config(cs)
        args = ["add"]
        for t in tags:
            args += ["-t", t]
        args += ["-a", "test", "--", "echo"]
        ok, info = compare(cr, cs, args)
        if not ok:
            failed += 1
            if len(fail_examples) < 15:
                fail_examples.append({
                    'kind': 'tags',
                    'value': tags,
                    'r_code': info[0], 's_code': info[1],
                    'r_err': info[4][:200].decode(errors='replace'),
                    's_err': info[5][:200].decode(errors='replace'),
                    'cr': info[6][:300].decode(errors='replace'),
                    'cs': info[7][:300].decode(errors='replace'),
                })
        else:
            passed += 1
        os.unlink(cr); os.unlink(cs)

    print(f"Passed: {passed}/{passed+failed}")
    for ex in fail_examples:
        print(json.dumps(ex, indent=2))


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main(int(sys.argv[1]) if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 100)

# Test config file lookup priority
test_resolve() {
    local name="$1"; shift
    local home="$1"; shift
    local xdg="$1"; shift
    local pier="$1"; shift
    local cwd="$1"; shift
    
    # Args: rest is pier args
    pushd "$cwd" > /dev/null
    if [ -z "$xdg" ]; then xdg_e="env -u XDG_CONFIG_HOME"; else xdg_e="XDG_CONFIG_HOME=$xdg"; fi
    if [ -z "$pier" ]; then pier_e="env -u PIER_CONFIG_PATH"; else pier_e="PIER_CONFIG_PATH=$pier"; fi
    
    HOME="$home" $xdg_e $pier_e $REF "$@" > /tmp/r_o 2>/tmp/r_e; rc=$?
    HOME="$home" $xdg_e $pier_e $SUT "$@" > /tmp/s_o 2>/tmp/s_e; sc=$?
    popd > /dev/null
    
    if [ "$rc" = "$sc" ] && diff -q /tmp/r_o /tmp/s_o > /dev/null && diff -q /tmp/r_e /tmp/s_e > /dev/null; then
        echo "PASS: $name"
    else
        echo "FAIL: $name (rc=$rc sc=$sc)"
        diff /tmp/r_o /tmp/s_o | head -3 | sed 's/^/  out: /'
        diff /tmp/r_e /tmp/s_e | head -3 | sed 's/^/  err: /'

# Save command in env var with trailing newline preserved
cmd_with_nl="$(printf 'multi\ncmd\n')"
echo "len of cmd: ${#cmd_with_nl}"
# Hmm bash strips trailing newlines from $(...)
printf 'multi\ncmd\n' > /tmp/cmd_file
echo "file len: $(wc -c < /tmp/cmd_file)"

# Run with command from file
read -d '' cmd < /tmp/cmd_file || true
echo "var len: ${#cmd}"
/bin/bash -c "$cmd" 2>&1 | od -c | tail -3
echo "---"
# Now use a different approach: use exec
/bin/bash -c "$(cat /tmp/cmd_file)" 2>&1 | od -c | tail -3

import subprocess

"

cmd_val = '$cmd_val'.encode().decode('unicode_escape')
import toml
t = {'scripts': {'foo': {'command': cmd_val}}, 'default': {}}
with open('/tmp/cmd_var_r.toml', 'w') as f:
    f.write('[scripts.foo]\ncommand = ' + repr(cmd_val).replace(chr(10), '\\\\n') + '\n\n[default]\n')

    echo ""
done

# Use direct python instead
for cmd_val in 'multi\ncmd' 'multi\ncmd\n' 'echo a\necho b' 'echo a\necho b\n'; do
    python3 -c "
import sys
cmd_val = sys.argv[1].encode().decode('unicode_escape')
# Write a simple TOML with escape-quoted string
s = '[scripts.foo]\ncommand = \"' + cmd_val.replace('\\\\','\\\\\\\\').replace('\"','\\\\\"').replace('\n','\\\\n') + '\"\n\n[default]\n'
with open('/tmp/cmd_var.toml','w') as f:
    f.write(s)
" "$cmd_val"
    echo "=== cmd: '$cmd_val' ==="
    echo "cnf:"
    cat /tmp/cmd_var.toml
    cp /tmp/cmd_var.toml /tmp/cv_r.toml

    echo "SUT run stderr only:"
    PIER_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/cv_s.toml $SUT run foo 2>&1 >/dev/null | od -c
done

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Markov-chain-style usage simulation: 10 fresh usage paths."""

import os, random, string, subprocess, sys, tempfile

REF = "/workspace/executable"
SUT = "/work/work/executable"


def run(binary, conf, args, timeout=10, stdin=None):
    env = dict(os.environ, PIER_CONFIG_PATH=conf)
    try:
        p = subprocess.run([binary] + args, env=env, capture_output=True,
                           input=stdin, timeout=timeout)
        return p.returncode, p.stdout, p.stderr
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
        return -1, b"", b"TIMEOUT"


def session_basic_usage(seed):
    """Typical user session: add, list, run, edit description, remove."""
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    cr = tempfile.mktemp(suffix=".toml")
    cs = tempfile.mktemp(suffix=".toml")
    with open(cr, 'w') as f:
        f.write("[default]\n")
    with open(cs, 'w') as f:
        f.write("[default]\n")

    sequence = []
    aliases = []
    for i in range(rng.randint(5, 12)):
        choice = rng.choices(['add', 'list', 'run', 'show', 'remove', 'copy', 'move'],
                              weights=[3, 2, 2, 1, 1, 1, 1])[0]
        if choice == 'add' or not aliases:
            a = ''.join(rng.choices(string.ascii_lowercase, k=rng.randint(2, 8)))
            cmd = rng.choice(['echo hello', 'pwd', 'true', 'false', 'exit 0', 'echo $@'])
            opts = []
            if rng.random() < 0.5: opts += ['-d', 'description']
            if rng.random() < 0.3: opts += ['-t', 'mytag']
            sequence.append(['add'] + opts + ['-a', a, '--', cmd])
            aliases.append(a)
        elif choice == 'list':
            opts = rng.choice([[], ['-q'], ['-l'], ['-c', '10']])
            sequence.append(['list'] + opts)
        elif choice == 'run':
            sequence.append(['run', rng.choice(aliases)])
        elif choice == 'show':
            sequence.append(['show', rng.choice(aliases)])
        elif choice == 'remove' and len(aliases) > 1:
            a = rng.choice(aliases)
            sequence.append(['remove', a])
            aliases.remove(a)
        elif choice == 'copy':
            a = rng.choice(aliases)
            new = a + '-copy'
            if new not in aliases:
                sequence.append(['copy', a, new])
                aliases.append(new)
        elif choice == 'move' and len(aliases) > 1:
            a = rng.choice(aliases)
            new = a + '-mv'
            if new not in aliases:
                sequence.append(['move', a, new])
                aliases.remove(a)
                aliases.append(new)

    return cr, cs, sequence


def main():
    failures = 0
    for seed in range(1, 11):
        cr, cs, ops = session_basic_usage(seed)
        diverged_at = None
        for i, op in enumerate(ops):
            rc, ro, re = run(REF, cr, op)
            sc, so, se = run(SUT, cs, op)
            with open(cr, 'rb') as f: crd = f.read()
            with open(cs, 'rb') as f: csd = f.read()
            if rc != sc or ro != so or re != se or crd != csd:
                diverged_at = (i, op, rc, sc, ro, so, re, se)
                break
        os.unlink(cr); os.unlink(cs)
        if diverged_at:
            i, op, rc, sc, ro, so, re, se = diverged_at
            print(f"SESSION {seed}: DIVERGED at op {i}: {op}")
            print(f"  rc={rc} sc={sc}")
            print(f"  ro={ro[:100]!r}")
            print(f"  so={so[:100]!r}")
            print(f"  re={re[:100]!r}")
            print(f"  se={se[:100]!r}")
            failures += 1
        else:
            print(f"SESSION {seed}: PASS ({len(ops)} ops)")
    print(f"\nTotal: {10 - failures}/10 sessions passed")


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

content.text:
The reimplementation of pier is complete and certified:

**Summary:**
- Recovered the pier 0.1.6 spec via black-box probing (CLI help, file I/O behavior, error messages, table rendering, TOML serialization).
- Wrote a Rust reimplementation in `/work/work/src/main.rs` using vendored crates (clap 2.33.0, toml 0.4.10, serde 1.0.89, snafu 0.5.0, dirs 2.0.2). The `vendor/` directory holds offline copies of all transitive dependencies (with stub crates for Windows/Redox/Hermit targets).
- Implemented all subcommands (add/list/run/show/remove/edit/copy/move/config-init) with their aliases (ls/rm/cp/mv/rename/init), the custom prettytable format with `≖` borders and `⋮` separators, the toml-rs 0.4 string-serialization heuristic, the shell invocation pattern `<interpreter> <command> <alias> [args]...`, and the verbose run decorations.
- Verified through 1700+ random differential tests, 57 string edge cases, 15 help-text comparisons, and a 10-session Markov-chain top-up — all matching the reference byte-for-byte.

Build: `/work/work/compile.sh` (cargo build --release --offline) produces `/work/work/executable` in ~8 seconds.
